edge_hypothesis: EMA crossover is marginal at standard R:R (best PF=1.073, typical PF=0.99) with persistent DD blowouts. However, EMA crossover has NEVER been tested with extreme R:R — the universal principle that improved MACD PF monotonically from 1.347 (3.5:1) to 1.353 (5:1) to 1.442 (8:1). EMA crossover produces the HIGHEST trade count of any template (183-389 trades on ETH/4h), which means MORE opportunities to capture fat tails. The DD blowout problem is caused by holding losing trades too long at standard R:R. With tight stops (1.0-1.5x ATR), each losing trade is cut quickly — the DD comes from MANY small losses, not a few large ones. At 6:1 R:R with 1.5x stop, breakeven win rate is only 14%. EMA_9/EMA_21 crossovers capture momentum shifts — not every shift leads to a sustained trend, but in crypto, the ones that DO sustain produce 6-8x ATR moves regularly (trend continuations after momentum flips). If even 20% of EMA crosses produce 6x ATR continuation moves, the strategy is profitable. This is a controlled rehabilitation test: if extreme R:R cannot rescue EMA crossover — the most signal-rich template — then the template should be permanently retired. Invalidation: (1) if PF < 1.0 at all R:R levels tested (4:1, 6:1, 8:1), the EMA crossover signal quality is too low for ANY R:R to compensate — retire permanently; (2) if PF improves monotonically with R:R (matching MACD pattern), the extreme R:R principle is template-universal.

variants.name: ema_crossover_moderate_rr_4to1
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on ETH/4h with 4:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 6.0x TP). Control variant — least extreme R:R tested. At breakeven win rate of 20%, this is achievable if even a moderate fraction of EMA crosses produce trend continuations. Uses the 1.5x ATR safety-floor stop. This establishes the baseline for monotonic R:R improvement testing on EMA crossover.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (fast EMA bullish crossover).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (fast EMA bearish crossover).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (6.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 4:1. Needs 20% win rate to break even after fees.
Control variant: establishes baseline for R:R monotonic improvement test
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 6.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 and EMA_21 crossover — highest signal count template (183-389 trades)
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 6.0x TP — 4:1 R:R control variant

variants.name: ema_crossover_extreme_rr_6to1
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on ETH/4h with 6:1 R:R (1.5x stop / 9.0x TP). Mid-range extreme R:R test. At breakeven win rate of 14%, this is very achievable even with a noisy signal like EMA crossover. The safety-floor 1.5x stop gives trades room to breathe. 9.0x ATR TP targets major trend legs — on ETH/4h, ATR is ~$50-80, so TP is $450-720 of price movement, which occurs regularly during trend initiations that EMA crosses capture.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (fast EMA bullish crossover).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (fast EMA bearish crossover).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (9.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 6:1. Needs 14% win rate to break even after fees.
Mid-range variant: if 4:1 and 6:1 both profitable, confirms monotonic improvement
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 9.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 and EMA_21 crossover — testing extreme R:R rehabilitation
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 9.0x TP — targets major trend legs

variants.name: ema_crossover_ultimate_8to1
variants.description: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossover on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R (1.0x stop / 8.0x TP). Mirrors the MACD 8:1 ultra approach — tight 1.0x ATR stop cuts losers fast, 8.0x ATR TP captures fat tails. At breakeven win rate of 11.1%, even a low-quality signal like EMA crossover should produce enough winners. The MACD 8:1 ultra achieved 18.59% win rate and DD=7.1% — if EMA crossover achieves even 15% win rate at 8:1, it becomes viable. The tight stop also addresses the DD blowout problem: instead of one large DD event, losses are distributed as many small stops.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (fast EMA bullish crossover).
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (fast EMA bearish crossover).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
Ultimate variant: mirrors MACD 8:1 ultra approach. If PF > 1.0, extreme R:R is template-universal
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 and EMA_21 crossover — ultimate rehabilitation test
EMA_9/EMA_21 momentum shift — 8:1 R:R mirrors MACD ultra
